Standard Technical Specification for Sub- critical Thermal Power Project - 2x(500MW or above) Main Plant Package Section- 4 (Piping, Valves, Thermal Insulation and Miscellaneous Systems/ Equipment) 4 -33 vi) The hot lines shall be provided with spring supported hangers with flexible connections to permit axial and lateral movements. Flexibility analysis shall be carried out for pipelines which have cons iderable straight run and necessary loops/ expansion joint etc. shall be provided as may be necessary depending on layout. vii) Piping and fittings shall be manufactured by an approved manufacturer of repute. They should be truly cylindrical of clear internal diameter, of uniform thickness, smooth and strong, free from dents, cracks and holes and other defects. viii) For rubber lined ERW pipes, beads shall be removed. ix) For water pipes/ducts, air release valves shall be provided at highest point and bends/change of direction of flow. Sizing criteria for air release valves shall be generally on the basis of valve size to pipe diameter ratio of 1:8. Requirement shall be decided as per relevant code. 4.5.3 Material i) All materials shall be submitted with proper material test certificates. ii) Material test certificates shall carry proper heat number or other acceptable references to enable identification of the certificate that certifies the material. iii) Material of construction for pipes carrying various fluids shall be as follows: a) Raw water, clarified water, ASTMA-36/ASTM A-53 type 'E' Gr. B/ service water and pH corrected IS-3589 Gr. 410/ IS-1239 Heavy. DM water (CCW system) b) Sea water GRP/ FRP/ MSRL c) Demineralised water Stainless steel ERW for sizes above50 Nb. Seamless stainless steel to ASTM A312, Gr. 304 for sizes 50 Nb & below d) Drinking water ASTM A-53 type E Gr. B galvanized/ IS 1239 heavy galvanised/ IS 3589 galvanised. e) Instrument air & plant air. ASTM A-53 type E Gr. B galvanised/ IS 3589 Gr. 410 galvanised/IS 1239 heavy galvanised. f) Oil piping API 5L Gr. B iv) Alternate materials offered by Bidder agai nst those specified shall either be equal to or superior to those specified. The responsibility for establishing equality or superiority of the alternate materials offe red rests entirely with the Bidder and any standard code required for establishing the same shall be in English language.
